agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"HTTP ingestion endpoint","to":"internal event pipeline","status":"correct","issue":"Event receipt and forwarding works correctly under normal conditions"},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"data warehouse","status":"risk","issue":"Non-idempotent writes mean retries (from circuit breaker recovery, exponential backoff, or lost ACKs) silently create duplicate records. At 10k events/sec, even a 0.1% duplicate rate is 10 phantom events/sec compounding continuously."},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"dead-letter queue","status":"broken","issue":"DLQ is write-only — no consumer, no reprocessing, no alerting on depth. Failed events are silently lost. This is a data integrity hole disguised as a resilience feature."},{"from":"retry mechanism","to":"data warehouse","status":"risk","issue":"Exponential backoff retries without idempotency keys means every retry is a potential duplicate insert. The retry mechanism actively degrades data quality."}],"contract_compliance":"At-least-once delivery semantics are acknowledged but not compensated for. The system promises event delivery but silently delivers duplicates with no way to detect or deduplicate downstream. Any consumer of this data warehouse (dashboards, ML models, billing) will operate on inflated numbers.","data_flow_integrity":"Compromised. The happy path works, but every resilience mechanism (retries, circuit breaker recovery, at-least-once delivery) injects duplicates into the warehouse. The system is more likely to corrupt data BECAUSE it has good resilience — more retries means more duplicates."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["No logging of duplicate event detection (because there is none)","DLQ depth and age of oldest message not logged"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Strong for latency and throughput. Blind spot on data correctness.","gaps":["No metric for duplicate events written to warehouse","No metric for DLQ depth or consumer lag (no consumer exists)","No metric for event uniqueness ratio (written vs unique)","No alert on DLQ growth rate"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Good — OpenTelemetry spans cover request lifecycle","gaps":["Cannot trace a single event through retry cycles to determine if it was written multiple times","No trace correlation between original write and retry write of the same event"]},"debugging_capability":"Strong for latency and availability issues. Completely blind to data duplication — the on-call engineer at 3am has no way to know duplicates are accumulating, and no tooling to quantify or remediate them after the fact."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Data warehouse momentarily unavailable, circuit breaker opens then closes","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Circuit breaker recovery triggers retries of buffered events. Without idempotency keys, every recovered event is a potential duplicate. Add a unique event_id generated at ingestion time and use UPSERT or INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING at the warehouse."},{"scenario":"ACK lost between warehouse and service after successful write","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Event is retried and written again. This is the at-least-once guarantee working as designed — but without dedup, it guarantees data corruption. Idempotency key on event_id is the fix."},{"scenario":"DLQ fills up over days/weeks with no consumer","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Silent data loss. Events that failed all 3 retries go to DLQ and are never seen again. Deploy a DLQ consumer with alerting. At minimum: alert when DLQ depth > 0 for more than 5 minutes."},{"scenario":"Sustained warehouse degradation causing high retry rate","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Circuit breaker handles availability, but a degraded-but-responding warehouse (slow writes, partial failures) could cause massive duplicate accumulation before the breaker trips. Add duplicate rate monitoring."},{"scenario":"DLQ storage exhaustion","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No retention policy or size limit documented. Unbounded queue growth is an operational time bomb. Set TTL and max-size with alerts."}],"retry_config":"Exponential backoff with 3 retries — well configured but dangerous without idempotent writes. Each retry is a duplicate injection vector.","timeout_config":"Not explicitly assessed but load test at 10k/sec for 30min suggests timeouts are reasonable for normal operation.","circuit_breakers":"Present on warehouse writes. Good pattern, but recovery phase amplifies duplicate risk.","graceful_degradation":"Partial. Service degrades to DLQ on failure, but DLQ is a black hole with no consumer. Graceful degradation requires eventual recovery — this has none."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Blue/green with load balancer traffic switch provides instant rollback. This is well-designed.","monitoring_coverage":"Excellent for availability and latency. Zero coverage for data correctness — the single most important property of an analytics ingestion pipeline.","on_call_documentation":"On-call engineer has no runbook for: (1) quantifying duplicates after an incident, (2) deduplicating warehouse data retroactively, (3) processing or replaying DLQ events. These are the three most likely 3am scenarios.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":["DLQ consumer/reprocessor service"],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add idempotency key (event_id) to event schema and warehouse table","Add UPSERT or dedup constraint on warehouse table"],"infra_provisioning":["DLQ alerting rule (depth > 0 for 5min)","DLQ retention policy and max-size configuration","Dashboard panel for duplicate event rate"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":true,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":true,"details":"Adding a unique constraint or dedup index on the warehouse table is required for idempotent writes. On a high-volume clickstream table this could be a significant migration — needs to be assessed for lock duration and planned during low-traffic window."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Non-idempotent warehouse writes with at-least-once delivery guarantee","reason":"This is not a nice-to-have. The retry logic, circuit breaker recovery, and at-least-once semantics all actively inject duplicates into the warehouse. Every downstream consumer (dashboards, ML pipelines, billing reports) will operate on silently inflated data. The better the resilience works, the worse the data quality gets. Fix: generate a unique event_id at ingestion, add a unique constraint on the warehouse, use UPSERT.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Dead-letter queue has no consumer, no alerting, no reprocessing","reason":"A DLQ without a consumer is not a resilience feature — it is a data loss feature with extra steps. Failed events silently accumulate and are never recovered. At minimum: deploy a DLQ consumer, alert on queue depth, set retention policy. Without this, the service has no actual recovery path for failed writes.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Duplicate write risk is certain given at-least-once + non-idempotent writes","DLQ gap is clearly documented in the spec","Observability and deployment mechanisms are strong"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact duplicate rate under production traffic patterns (load test may not have triggered enough retries to surface this)","Warehouse table size and migration complexity for adding unique constraint"],"assumptions":["The data warehouse supports UPSERT or ON CONFLICT semantics","Event IDs can be generated or derived deterministically at ingestion time","Downstream consumers have no independent deduplication"]}}
